title: astragalus subsecundus
intro: Astragalus subsecundus, also known as the Mongolian milkvetch, is a type of legume native to China and Mongolia. It is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Chinese and Ayurvedic herbalism, for its adaptogenic and medicinal properties. Astragalus subsecundus is believed to have immune-boosting properties, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fatigue, anemia, and respiratory issues, while also being used as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction, capsule, or tea, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
